$comment: VENDORED COPY for the WS-A contract test in claude-copilot/tools/cc. Source of truth is copilot-control-tower/docs/01-architecture/schemas/auth.schema.json — update this copy from there, not the reverse.
title: copilot auth --json
description: GitHub device-flow sign-in seam (WS-A Stream-A). Three payload kinds discriminated by `kind`, plus the shared error envelope: `device-code` (`cc auth login --json` -- initiate), `poll` (`cc auth login --poll --device-code <code> --json` -- one poll step), and `status` (`cc auth status --json` -- offline-safe, no network). The OAuth access token this flow obtains is written directly to the per-user OS keychain by the CLI (claude-copilot/tools/cc/src/cc/core/keychain.py) and NEVER appears in any instance of this schema -- see the `allOf` NO-SECRET fitness invariant below.

user_code.description: The short code the user types at verification_uri.

verification_uri.description: Where the user completes sign-in in a browser.

interval.description: Minimum seconds to wait between poll attempts.

device_code.description: Short-lived flow handle the caller polls with next (`cc auth login --poll --device-code <this>`). NOT a credential by itself -- GitHub also requires the originating client_id on every poll, and it expires in `expires_in` seconds. Distinct from an OAuth access token, which this schema never carries (see the top-level NO-SECRET invariant).

status.description: Result of one GitHub device-flow poll step. `pending`: keep polling (covers both GitHub's authorization_pending and slow_down responses). `authorized`: sign-in succeeded -- the CLI stored the token directly in the OS keychain; it is NEVER present in this payload. `expired`: the device_code expired -- restart from `device-code`. `denied`: the user declined (or GitHub returned an otherwise-unrecognized terminal error, treated fail-closed as denied).
Computed OFFLINE-SAFE from the local non-secret identity pointer plus a keychain presence check only -- never a network call.

identity.description: Present only when status is authorized.

scope.description: The OAuth scopes granted at sign-in. Present only when status is authorized and a scope string was recorded at sign-in time; its absence must not be read as a granted-no-scopes signal.

status.$comment: `identity`/`scope` are optional even though `status` (which they depend on) is required at the object level -- a consumer must not assume presence just because status=authorized, but the CLI is expected to emit both whenever the local identity pointer has them.

errorEnvelope.description: Shared error envelope (cli-contract.md's uniform `{schema_version, error:{code,message}}` shape, exit 2). `no-company-app` is the auth-specific code: the org's GitHub App connection hasn't been set up yet (created during admin standup, not something an individual user can self-serve).

noSecretKeys.$comment: NO-SECRET fitness invariant, applied via the top-level `allOf`: recursively forbids access_token/token/refresh_token/secret as a property name at ANY nesting depth, in any object or array, anywhere in an `auth --json` instance. The OAuth token is written directly to the OS keychain by the CLI (claude-copilot/tools/cc/src/cc/core/keychain.py) and MUST NEVER appear in this contract -- see claude-copilot/tools/cc/tests/test_auth_contract.py's recursive NO-SECRET fitness test, which additionally asserts this against real build_auth_poll_report()/execute_auth_login() output (not just schema-shape fixtures) and against the keychain spy that actually received the token.
